title: The Past Explains the Present
description: On pages 17–18, this part focuses on the past explains the present, keeping the scene centered on the conflict, choices, and stakes of the Alabasta arc.
scene: The Past Explains the Present. Vivi’s childhood bond with Koza and the roots of the rebel movement give the civil war a personal face. This section presents the immediate scene beat before the dialogue continues.

dialogue.speaker: Vivi
dialogue.text: I tried and tried... Honest to god, I tried so hard to stop them...

dialogue.speaker: Luffy
dialogue.text: But nothing I said was of any use... Those rebels just won't stop...

dialogue.speaker: Luffy
dialogue.text: But their supplies are running low and they know they can't hold out for much longer...

dialogue.speaker: Nami
dialogue.text: That's why they're planning on settling things once and for all in their next offensive!

dialogue.speaker: Usopp
dialogue.text: They're practically suicidal at this point!

dialogue.speaker: Zoro
dialogue.text: You have to stop those fools...!

dialogue.speaker: Chopper
dialogue.text: Katorea Oasis Town (Situated next to Nanohana)

dialogue.speaker: Toto
dialogue.text: Current Main Base of the Rebels

dialogue.speaker: Koza
dialogue.text: It's not much but we managed to score this much at least... Where's Kouza?

dialogue.speaker: Nami
dialogue.text: What's the matter? You seem to be spacing out...

summary: This part develops the past explains the present and pushes the Alabasta conflict closer to its next decisive turn.
